title: Mole (mol)
short: The SI unit for amount of substance: one mole contains 6.022×10²³ particles (Avogadro's number).
full:
The mole is chemistry's counting unit. One mole of any substance contains Avogadro's number, 6.022×10²³, of its formula units — atoms, molecules or ions. It links the particle scale to the laboratory scale of grams and litres.

The molar mass (g/mol) converts between mass and moles, and for a gas the molar volume relates moles to volume. Concentration in mol/L (molarity) expresses how many moles of solute sit in each litre of solution, the everyday currency of equilibrium and acid–base calculations.
